code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1â˜… (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Premature failure (â‰¤6 months) with functional consequences
    - "Avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence
    - Negative updates erase previous satisfaction
    - Immediate disposal due to critical defects

    2â˜… (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification
    - Repeated failures within warranty period

    3â˜… (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Temporary solutions with acknowledged shortcomings

    4â˜… (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Functional limitation in primary use case (even if suitable elsewhere)
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full function
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Brand comparisons with minor shortcomings
    - Meeting expectations without enthusiastic endorsement
    - Brief praise with 1-2 benefits lacking enthusiasm

    5â˜… (Very Positive):
    - Solves persistent problems through daily reliability
    - 3+ practical benefits praised (durability/utility/cost-effectiveness)
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite imperfections
    - Targeted recommendations solving specific group needs
    - Replacement purchases/continuous use despite part replacements
    - Practical benefits outweigh multi-aspect criticisms

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Primary use limitation â†’4 even if suitable elsewhere
    - Praise + functional flaw â†’4 / + non-functional â†’5
    - "Works as intended" + validation â†’5 / neutral confirmation â†’4
    - Multi-aspect criticism + final endorsement â†’5
    - Cost-effective solutions for specific needs â†’5
    - Brief enthusiastic praise ("Perfect!") â†’5 / generic praise â†’4
    - Star rating mentions for aspects don't override holistic evaluation
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures â†’1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "This beautiful mug is too small for coffee - perfect for tea decor"
<Reasoning>Primary use limitation despite alternative suitability â†’4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Pyrex lids wore out but containers remain daily drivers"
<Reasoning>Continuous core satisfaction despite replacements â†’5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Stasher bag great but don't overstuff seal"
<Reasoning>Usage caveat limits functionality â†’4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Perfect magnets! Strong hold and ideal size PLUS easy installation"
<Reasoning>3 benefits + enthusiastic praise â†’5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Leaks constantly and customer service ignored me - regret buying"
<Reasoning>Core defect with regret statement â†’2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "UPDATE: Failed after 3mo. Never again this brand"
<Reasoning>Definitive failure update â†’1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Color isn't perfect but works amazingly well daily"
<Reasoning>Non-functional flaw + core praise â†’5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Decent performance but uncomfortable handles limit prolonged use"
<Reasoning>Balanced pros/cons with functional limitation â†’3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Mattress gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 for features - worth it overall"
<Reasoning>Multi-aspect praise outweighing single criticism â†’5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (32 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 12
  gold 5 -> 4: 11
  gold 1 -> 2: 4
  gold 1 -> 4: 1
  gold 3 -> 1: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "My first dispenser arrived shattered (it really seemed packed well), but a replacement arrived unscathed.  As others have noted, it looks much much better in person.  While a bottle is not included everything else is.  You'll need to install the spig..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This bathroom rug is so plush and soft. One of my dogs tried to claim it while I had it laid out on the couch. It is 100% organic cotton that is sustainable. So you help the environment and the natural resources. This rug is machine washable, gentle ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [5, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "This is a very pretty carpet runner and in real life it looks even better than the product photo.  The colors are rich and the pattern would go with a variety of design styles.

NOTES
==============
--this is a very flat carpet.
For my purposes in th..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "Made my first batch of hard boiled eggs and it was great! I didn't read the directions close enough and closed the lid....it is to remain just as a cover, not locked. The eggs were pretty easy to pierce and the best part was that after they were done..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "While this looks cool, it functionally is no good.  To grate cheese, you have to hold onto the top of the taco.  Because it is plastic, the two sides squeeze together when gripped. This, coupled with the slipperiness of the plastic, makes this thing ..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 1, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "Didn't work. Had to send it back."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 1, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "On my computer screen the color for the 'cool brown' bath mat is accurate.  It's a faded sort of brown with lots of gray tint to it.  Our upstairs bath is in browns -- golden-hue brown and tile brown-- and it fits in.

What you get is a cool-brown no..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 5] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 32 errors, 19 borderline out of 80 examples."}
